# Contributing to Pikos

## Issues — welcome

Bug reports and feature requests are genuinely helpful. Use the issue templates or file a blank issue if they don't fit.

You can also:
- Report a bug or send feedback at [pikos.app/feedback](https://pikos.app/feedback)
- Send feedback directly to [hello@pikos.app](mailto:hello@pikos.app)

## Pull requests — not accepted

Pikos is source-available so you can verify the privacy claims and inspect the code. It is not an open-source collaboration project.

**Pull requests will be automatically closed.** This isn't personal — it's practical:

- Pikos is built and maintained by one person. Reviewing external code takes more time than writing the fix directly.
- The codebase has strict conventions (React Compiler, no useMemo/useCallback, specific testing patterns) that are hard to follow without deep context.
- The license (BUSL) requires careful handling of contributed code. Accepting PRs creates ownership ambiguity without a CLA, and adding a CLA creates friction that isn't worth it at this scale.

If you've found a bug and know the fix, describe it in an issue — what's wrong, where it is, and what the fix looks like. That's just as useful as a PR and faster for everyone.

This policy may change in the future. For now, issues are the best way to contribute.
